column5: Portland's eastside suburbs stretch from the Willamette River cliffs of Milwaukie all the way out to the foothills of Happy Valley. You'll find a wide range of housing prices here, along with strong community identities, river access, and growing retail and dining scenes.
column6: Portland Eastside Suburbs | Real Estate & Community Guides
column8: Browse Portland's eastside suburbs including Happy Valley, Milwaukie, Gresham, Oregon City, and Clackamas. Find homes and local guides for every eastside...

column5: Marion County anchors the heart of the Willamette Valley, with Salem serving as Oregon's state capital. The cities here offer affordability relative to the Portland metro, strong agricultural roots, and a growing economy. A solid option for buyers who want more house for their dollar.
column6: Marion County Cities | Willamette Valley Real Estate Guide
column8: Find homes in Marion County from Salem and Keizer to Woodburn and Stayton. Explore communities, schools, and real estate across the Willamette Valley.

column5: Yamhill County is Oregon wine country at its finest. The cities here sit among rolling vineyards, historic downtowns, and quiet agricultural land. If you're looking for a slower pace without being too far from Portland, this corner of the Willamette Valley is worth a serious look.
column6: Yamhill County Cities | Oregon Wine Country Real Estate
column8: Explore homes in Yamhill County, Oregon's premier wine region. Browse Newberg, McMinnville, Carlton, and Dundee with local guides and current listings.

column5: The westside suburbs offer a mix of high-ranking schools, tech industry jobs, and easy MAX light rail access. From the lakefront living and top-rated schools of Lake Oswego to the small-town charm and parks of Sherwood, this side of the metro offers a wide range of home styles and price points.
column6: Portland Westside Suburbs | Real Estate & Community Guides
column8: Explore Portland's westside suburbs including Lake Oswego, Beaverton, Hillsboro, Sherwood, and Tualatin. Find listings, guides, and local market data for...

column5: Your starting point for exploring every city and community in the Portland metro area. Whether you're drawn to westside tech corridors, eastside river towns, Yamhill wine country, or Marion County's capital region, this hub connects you to all the local guides you need.
column6: Portland Metro Cities & Real Estate | Find Your Community
column8: Explore every city and neighborhood in the Portland metro area. Compare communities, schools, and home prices to find the right place to call home.

column5: West Linn sits on the bluffs above the Willamette River and is known for excellent schools, tight-knit neighborhoods, and a close-knit community with well-maintained streets and strong neighborhood involvement. It's a bit removed from the urban core, which is exactly the point for most buyers who choose it. Expect well-maintained homes and neighbors who tend to stay put.
column6: West Linn Homes for Sale | West Linn, OR Real Estate
column8: West Linn offers scenic river views, top-tier schools, and quiet residential living on Portland's southside. Browse current West Linn listings here.

column5: Tualatin is a practical, livable suburb that often gets overlooked in favor of its flashier neighbors. Buyers who look here find solid value, good schools, easy I-5 access, and a community centered around the Tualatin River parks network. A smart pick for commuters heading north or south.
column6: Tualatin Homes for Sale | Tualatin, OR Real Estate
column8: Tualatin sits at the crossroads of Washington and Clackamas counties with community parks, strong schools, and easy commuting from I-5.

column5: Troutdale bills itself as the Gateway to the Gorge, and it earns that title. From here you're minutes from the Historic Columbia River Highway, Multnomah Falls, and some of the best hiking in the state. The town has a charming historic district and the iconic McMenamins Edgefield nearby. Buyers here tend to prioritize outdoor access above everything else.
column6: Troutdale Homes for Sale | Troutdale, OR Real Estate
column8: Troutdale is the gateway to the Columbia River Gorge with outdoor access, historic character, and some of the most affordable homes on the eastside.

column5: Tigard is a practical, well-connected westside suburb with excellent schools, Cook Park along the Tualatin River, and a downtown revitalization bringing new energy to the city. Buyers find solid value here with easy I-5 and Highway 99W access for commuting in any direction.
column6: Tigard Homes for Sale | Tigard, OR Real Estate
column8: Tigard offers fantastic schools, Cook Park, and easy I-5 access at prices below Portland proper. Explore current homes for sale in Tigard, OR.

column5: Sherwood has grown significantly over the past two decades but still holds onto its small-town personality. You'll find newer construction mixed with established neighborhoods, easy access to Yamhill County wine country, and one of the more walkable downtowns with local shops and community events on the westside. The Robin Hood Festival is a beloved community tradition.
column6: Sherwood Homes for Sale | Sherwood, OR Real Estate
column8: Sherwood offers top-rated schools, a walkable Old Town, and easy access to wine country. Great schools, quiet neighborhoods, and easy freeway access make it a top pick.

column6: Portland Homes for Sale | Portland, OR Real Estate
column8: Browse all homes for sale in Portland, Oregon. Updated daily with photos, pricing, and neighborhood details to help you find the right fit.

column5: Oregon City holds a special place in Pacific Northwest history as the first incorporated city west of the Rockies. Today it's a city of distinct districts: the historic Hilltop connected by one of the only remaining municipal elevators in the country, the McLoughlin neighborhood along the Willamette, and newer residential areas spreading south. Buyers here get history, character, and solid value.
column6: Oregon City Neighborhoods | OC Real Estate Guide
column8: Explore Oregon City's distinct neighborhoods from the historic McLoughlin District to the hilltop areas. Find homes and community info across all of Oregon...

column5: Milwaukie punches above its weight. It's small in size but big on personality, with a thriving local food scene, easy MAX Orange Line access into downtown Portland, and genuine neighborhood pride. Home prices are more approachable than many inner Portland neighborhoods while keeping you close to the city. A favorite among buyers who want urban energy with a residential feel.
column6: Milwaukie Homes for Sale | Milwaukie, OR Real Estate
column8: Milwaukie offers riverfront charm, MAX light rail access, and some of the best value on the eastside. Browse homes in this underrated Clackamas city.

column5: Lake Oswego is one of the most sought-after addresses in the Portland metro. Built around a private lake and anchored by consistently top-ranked schools, LO blends boutique shopping and locally owned restaurants, beautiful parks, and a genuine small-town feel with high home values to match. A great fit for buyers who prioritize schools and lifestyle.
column6: Lake Oswego Homes for Sale | Lake Oswego, OR Real Estate
column8: Lake Oswego is one of Portland's highest-valued residential areas with top-ranked schools, lake access, and a thriving luxury market. Start your search here.

column5: Hillsboro is the tech engine of the Portland metro. Intel's massive campus is here, along with dozens of other high-tech employers. The city has invested heavily in its downtown, expanded its trail system, and built out solid MAX transit. Housing is more affordable than Lake Oswego or West Linn, making it attractive for first-time buyers in the tech sector.
column6: Hillsboro Homes for Sale | Hillsboro, OR Real Estate
column8: Hillsboro is Oregon's tech hub, home to Intel, Nike, and more. Find homes from Orenco Station to South Hillsboro at a range of prices.

column5: Happy Valley is one of the fastest-growing cities in Oregon and for good reason. The schools consistently rank among the best in the state, the homes are newer and larger than average, and the hillside setting gives many properties sweeping views of Mt. Hood. It tends to attract families who want space, safety, and strong education options.
column6: Happy Valley Homes for Sale | Happy Valley, OR Real Estate
column8: Happy Valley is one of Oregon's fastest-growing cities, known for custom builds, top schools, hilltop views, and a thriving residential community.

column5: Gresham is the fourth-largest city in Oregon and offers some of the best affordability in the metro. It has its own downtown, a vibrant arts community, and direct MAX Blue Line access into Portland. The housing stock ranges from mid-century ranches to newer construction, and the city continues to invest in revitalization. A strong value play for buyers priced out of closer-in neighborhoods.
column6: Gresham Homes for Sale | Gresham, OR Real Estate
column8: Gresham is Portland's largest eastside suburb with MAX light rail, affordable prices, and diverse neighborhoods that offer real value for buyers.

column5: Forest Grove has a small-college-town energy anchored by Pacific University. It sits at the edge of wine country and offers some of the most affordable single-family homes on the westside. If you don't mind being a bit further from the urban core, you'll find great value, a historic downtown, and beautiful views of the Coast Range.
column6: Forest Grove Homes for Sale | Forest Grove, OR Real Estate
column8: Forest Grove is home to Pacific University, scenic wine country access, and a historic downtown with a growing residential market worth exploring.

column5: Fairview is a small gem tucked between Gresham and Troutdale on the eastern edge of the metro. It wraps around scenic Blue Lake Regional Park and offers quiet residential streets, affordable prices, and easy I-84 access for commuters. If you want to be close to the Columbia River Gorge without paying premium prices, Fairview is worth exploring.
column6: Fairview Homes for Sale | Fairview, OR Real Estate
column8: Fairview is a small, charming eastside community near Gresham with lakefront living, quiet streets, and prices that buyers in tighter markets appreciate.

column5: Damascus is a unique community that resisted incorporation for years, preserving its rural character even as surrounding suburbs built out. You'll find a mix of farmland, large-lot homes, and newer residential developments. If you want acreage within reasonable distance of Portland, Damascus deserves a serious look.
column6: Damascus Homes for Sale | Damascus, OR Real Estate
column8: Damascus offers larger lots, rural character, and custom home potential just minutes from the amenities of Happy Valley and Clackamas.

column5: Gladstone sits at the confluence of the Clackamas and Willamette rivers, giving it some of the best outdoor recreation access in the metro. It's a small city with a strong community identity, older housing stock, and prices that still offer real value. Buyers who enjoy kayaking, fishing, or just being near the water love it here.
column6: Gladstone Homes for Sale | Gladstone, OR Real Estate
column8: Gladstone is a tight-knit Clackamas community with river access, tree-lined streets, and home prices that still make sense for first-time buyers.

column5: Clackamas is a major retail and commercial hub for the southeast Portland metro, built around Clackamas Town Center and easy I-205 access. Neighborhoods here are quiet and residential, schools are solid, and you'll find more square footage for your dollar than in many closer-in Portland areas. Good option for buyers who want suburban convenience without sacrificing proximity to the city.
column6: Clackamas Homes for Sale | Clackamas, OR Real Estate
column8: Clackamas is an eastside hub with easy mall access, strong freeway connections, and a mix of established neighborhoods and newer residential options.

column5: Salem is Oregon's state capital and a fully developed city in its own right. It has its own economy, distinct neighborhoods, a thriving arts and dining scene, and Willamette University in the heart of downtown. Buyers who move from Portland typically find they can buy significantly more home for their money without sacrificing urban amenities.
column6: Salem Homes for Sale | Salem, OR Real Estate
column8: Salem is Oregon's state capital with diverse neighborhoods, strong employment, and a variety of homes at prices well below the Portland metro.

column5: Beaverton is one of the most dynamic suburbs in the region, home to Nike's world headquarters and a thriving tech sector. The city has a remarkably diverse population, a growing restaurant scene, strong schools, and some of the best walkability on the westside. Whether you're buying your first home or upsizing, Beaverton has options across every price point.
column6: Beaverton Homes for Sale | Beaverton, OR Real Estate
column8: Beaverton is the heart of Oregon's Silicon Forest with excellent schools, a diverse housing market, and prices below Lake Oswego. Find your Beaverton home here.

column5: Aloha is an unincorporated community sitting between Beaverton and Hillsboro, which means it often gets overlooked despite offering real value. You'll find a mix of older ranch homes and newer builds, competitive prices, and solid proximity to both tech employers and westside amenities. A practical choice for buyers watching their budget.
column6: Aloha, OR Homes for Sale | Washington County Real Estate
column8: Find homes in Aloha, OR, a community between Beaverton and Hillsboro with affordable prices, good schools, and easy tech-corridor access.

column5: Dundee is tiny in population but massive in reputation among wine lovers. The Dundee Hills AVA is home to some of Oregon's most celebrated wineries, and the town sits right in the middle of all of it. If waking up to vineyard views and having world-class wine within walking distance sounds appealing, Dundee delivers that lifestyle at a surprisingly accessible price point.
column6: Dundee Homes for Sale | Dundee, OR Real Estate
column8: Dundee sits in the heart of the Willamette Valley wine region with vineyard views, a small-town feel, and a uniquely Oregon lifestyle.

column5: Amity is small and agricultural, sitting in the heart of the Eola-Amity Hills AVA, one of Oregon's premier wine-growing regions. It's the kind of place where everybody knows their neighbors, the school district is tight-knit, and you can be at a world-class winery in five minutes. A strong fit for buyers seeking rural lifestyle with wine country access.
column6: Amity Homes for Sale | Amity, OR Real Estate
column8: Amity is a quiet Yamhill County community surrounded by vineyards with a small-town feel and access to some of Oregon's most scenic wine country.

column5: Carlton calls itself the Wine Capital of Oregon and with good reason. The town is surrounded by top-tier wineries and has a tasting room scene right on its main street. Despite all that, home prices remain surprisingly reasonable. It has become a destination for buyers who want wine country living without the Napa price tag.
column6: Carlton Homes for Sale | Carlton, OR Real Estate
column8: Carlton is known as Oregon's Wine Capital with walkable tasting rooms, a charming downtown, and a tight-knit community that draws wine-country buyers.

column5: Sheridan is a small, working-class community in the South Yamhill River valley with an honest, unpretentious character. It's more affordable than almost anywhere else in the county and draws buyers who want rural proximity, a slower pace, and land they can actually do something with.
column6: Sheridan Homes for Sale | Sheridan, OR Real Estate
column8: Sheridan is a small Yamhill County city with affordable rural-suburban real estate and a quiet, community-centered lifestyle.

column5: Stayton is the gateway to the Santiam Canyon and all the recreation that comes with it: fishing on the North Santiam River, trails into the Cascades, and access to Detroit Lake. The town itself has a quiet, small-community feel with solid local schools. A great fit for buyers who want outdoor access baked into their daily life.
column6: Stayton Homes for Sale | Stayton, OR Real Estate
column8: Stayton is the gateway to the Santiam Canyon with a small-town feel, community events, and affordable homes in the heart of the valley.

column5: Aumsville is a small residential community southeast of Salem with a peaceful, rural-suburban character. It's the kind of place where families have deep roots and new buyers are welcomed into an established community. Home prices are among the most affordable in the region, making it a solid entry point for first-time buyers.
column6: Aumsville Homes for Sale | Aumsville, OR Real Estate
column8: Aumsville is a quiet, residential Marion County community with affordable homes and a peaceful small-town lifestyle near Salem.

column5: Turner is a small city just south of Salem with a quiet, almost forgotten quality that many residents love. It's close enough to Salem for easy commuting but far enough to feel removed from the city. Older homes, larger lots, and very competitive prices make it worth considering for buyers who don't need to be in the middle of everything.
column6: Turner Homes for Sale | Turner, OR Real Estate
column8: Turner is a charming small city just south of Salem with a close-knit community feel and affordable residential options for valley buyers.

column5: Mount Angel wears its Bavarian heritage proudly. The famous Oktoberfest draws over 100,000 visitors each year, and the hilltop Benedictine Abbey is one of the most architecturally significant buildings in the state. Day-to-day life here is quiet and community-focused, with an agricultural backdrop and very accessible housing prices.
column6: Mount Angel Homes for Sale | Mount Angel, OR Real Estate
column8: Mount Angel is famous for its Bavarian heritage and annual Oktoberfest, offering charming homes and a uniquely cultural community experience.

column5: Aurora is a designated National Historic District and walking its streets feels like stepping back into the 19th century. The town was founded as a Christian communal colony in the 1850s and the original buildings have been beautifully preserved. Antique shops line the main street. If you want to live somewhere with genuine historical depth, Aurora is one of a kind.
column6: Aurora Homes for Sale | Aurora, OR Real Estate
column8: Aurora is a historic Willamette Valley town with a nationally recognized antique district, charming properties, and small-town Oregon character.

column5: Gervais is a tiny agricultural community in the northern Willamette Valley with very low profile and very affordable prices. It's surrounded by farmland, close to I-5 for commuters, and has the kind of unhurried pace that's hard to find anywhere near a major metro. A straightforward option for buyers who want affordability above everything else.
column6: Gervais Homes for Sale | Gervais, OR Real Estate
column8: Gervais is a small Marion County community with a quiet residential feel, affordable homes, and easy access to both Salem and the I-5 corridor.

column5: Scotts Mills is one of the smallest communities in Marion County, nestled in the foothills of the Cascades near the North Fork of the Molalla River. It's rural to its core with a tiny population, but buyers who find it are typically looking for exactly that: land, quiet, and a genuine foothill setting away from suburban sprawl.
column6: Scotts Mills Homes for Sale | Scotts Mills, OR Real Estate
column8: Scotts Mills is a rural Marion County community known for its scenic countryside, quiet lifestyle, and access to Oregon's forest lands.

column5: The city of Yamhill is a quiet, rural community surrounded by farmland and vineyards with a genuine small-town pace. Buyers who choose Yamhill are typically looking for land, privacy, and distance from city noise, and they find all three. A tight-knit local character you won't find in the suburbs.
column6: Yamhill Homes for Sale | Yamhill, OR Real Estate
column8: Yamhill is a rural-suburban community in Yamhill County with a small-town character and easy access to wine country living.

column5: McMinnville consistently tops lists of best small cities in Oregon, and it earns it. The downtown Third Street corridor is packed with award-winning restaurants, independent shops, and tasting rooms. It's the hub of the Willamette Valley wine industry. A genuinely special place to live if you love food, wine, and community.
column6: McMinnville Homes for Sale | McMinnville, OR Real Estate
column8: McMinnville is the cultural heart of Oregon wine country with an award-winning downtown, diverse housing options, and a growing economy.

column5: Keizer sits directly north of Salem and functions as its more suburban, residential, community-focused neighbor. The schools are strong, neighborhoods are clean and well-maintained, and home prices tend to be slightly more accessible than Salem proper. Volcanoes Stadium and Keizer Rapids Park give the community a genuine local identity.
column6: Keizer Homes for Sale | Keizer, OR Real Estate
column8: Keizer is a growing community just north of Salem with well-kept neighborhoods with parks and community programs, good schools, and affordable suburban living in the valley.

column5: Lafayette is a small historic city with one of the most charming old downtown corridors in the county, including the famous Lafayette Schoolhouse Antique Mall. The surrounding neighborhoods have a genuine warmth to them. Affordable, characterful, and more accessible than you'd expect given the quality of life.
column6: Lafayette Homes for Sale | Lafayette, OR Real Estate
column8: Lafayette is one of Yamhill County's fastest-growing historic towns with a welcoming community, affordable homes, and wine country charm.

column5: Newberg is the largest city in Yamhill County and a true gateway to Oregon wine country. George Fox University anchors a college-town energy here, and the downtown has a genuine dining and arts scene. Home prices are considerably lower than Portland metro averages, and you're surrounded by world-class Pinot Noir vineyards. Growing fast, so buyers who move now tend to be glad they did.
column6: Newberg Homes for Sale | Newberg, OR Real Estate
column8: Newberg is the gateway to Yamhill County wine country with a growing downtown, top schools, and a community feel that draws buyers from across the region.

column5: Hubbard is a quiet farming community best known for its annual Hop Festival celebrating the region's agricultural heritage. Life here is genuinely slow and community-driven, with a mixture of longtime farming families and newer residents drawn by affordable prices and a rural setting. About 25 miles from Portland, it's a real commute but a real escape.
column6: Hubbard Homes for Sale | Hubbard, OR Real Estate
column8: Hubbard is a small, affordable Marion County town with affordable housing, a welcoming community, and convenient access to Woodburn and Salem.

column5: Woodburn is known for its premium outlets, but the city itself is a growing community with a strong Hispanic cultural presence that has shaped its food scene, festivals, and character. It also has a well-regarded active adult community in Woodburn Estates. Affordable housing and a central Valley location between Portland and Salem make it practical for commuters.
column6: Woodburn Homes for Sale | Woodburn, OR Real Estate
column8: Woodburn is a growing Marion County city with active adult communities, a diverse population, and affordable homes south of the Portland metro.

column5: Ashcreek is a quiet residential neighborhood near the Tigard border in SW Portland. It has a suburban feel with well-maintained homes, good schools, and proximity to both Multnomah Village amenities and the commercial areas of Tigard. A practical, livable neighborhood without a lot of flash, which is exactly what many buyers are looking for.

column5: Arnold Creek is a scenic, wooded SW neighborhood on the edge of Tryon Creek State Natural Area. Homes here often back up to or overlook the forest, giving residents a nature-immersive experience that's rare in a city setting. Larger lots, a quieter pace, and strong access to hiking trails make it a great fit for outdoor-focused buyers.
column6: Arnold Creek Homes for Sale | SW Portland
column8: Find homes in Arnold Creek, SW Portland. A scenic neighborhood with larger lots and direct access to Tryon Creek State Park's trails.

column5: South Waterfront is Portland's most modern neighborhood, built from scratch in the 2000s on former industrial land along the Willamette. It has sleek high-rise condos, the aerial tram connecting to OHSU above, and a riverfront esplanade that makes it genuinely walkable. Popular with healthcare professionals and buyers who want modern construction and river views in an urban setting.

column8: Search homes in South Waterfront, Portland's most modern urban neighborhood. High-rise condos, riverfront living, and the OHSU aerial tram nearby.

column8: Find homes in Hillsdale, SW Portland. A walkable community centered on the Hillsdale town center, a beloved food cart pod, and PCC Sylvania.

column8: Search homes in Homestead, SW Portland. Near OHSU and Marquam Nature Park with wooded lots and sweeping Portland skyline views.

column8: Search homes in Markham, SW Portland. Residential listings near the Maricara Natural Area with quick I-5 access and an established neighborhood feel.
